channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Esports Nations Cup Completes List of Games with Street Fighter 6
item.content:encoded:
The list of titles for the inaugural Esports Nations Cup (ENC) is complete following the announcement that Street Fighter 6 will feature at the Riyadh event from November 18-22.

The Esports Foundation (fomerly the Esports World Cup Foundation) announced the addition, along with its format, detailing that it would consist of 4v4 Team Fights as teams try to win gold for their nations.

The complete list of Esports Nations Cup 2026 titles is:

  • Apex Legends
  • Chess
  • Counter-Strike 2
  • Dota 2
  • FATAL FURY: City of the Wolves
  • FC 26
  • Honor of Kings
  • League of Legends
  • Mobile Legends Bang Bang
  • PUBG Battlegrounds
  • PUBG Mobile
  • Rainbow Six Siege
  • Rocket League
  • Street Fighter 6
  • Trackmania
  • Valorant

Esports Nations Cup Street Fighter 6 dates, format, and qualification routes

Dates:

  • November 18-22

Format:

  • 24 teams
  • 4v4 Team Battle format – Four 1v1 matches where each player plays once
  • A 2-2 scoreline results in a sudden-death tiebreaker
  • Grand Final to be Best of Eight
  • Four six-team round-robin Groups
  • The top four of each group advance to a single-elimination playoff bracket

Qualification criteria:

  • 12 teams invited based on the ENC Street Fighter 6 National Team Ranking system
  • 10 teams qualify through regional qualifiers – South + East Asia (1 slot), Southeast Asia (1), Oceania (1), North America (1), Central America (1), South America (1), Western Europe (1), Eastern Europe (1), Middle East (1), Africa (1)
  • 2 Wildcard slots

Player criteria:

  • Invited nations & Wildcard teams: Consists of the top-ranked player from the 2026 World Warrior Ranking + three highest-ranked players from Street Fighters 6 National Team Ranking
  • Qualified Teams: Regional qualifier winner + the top-ranked player from the 2026 World Warrior Ranking + two highest-ranked players from Street Fighters 6 National Team Ranking
  • If a nation laccks enough ranked players to fill rtoster, the National Team Manager or Team Capttain may select remaining players subject to ENC approval.

Who do you think will dominate the SF6 ENC tournament? Talk about that on Insider Gaming’s Discord!
For more ENC news, see how ENC’s $45 million financial package will be distributed.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android

IEM Cologne 2026 Major: All Qualified Teams

The invites for the IEM Cologne 2026 Major are set following the whirlwind conclusion of local LANs as we approached the April 6 cutoff date.

Red Bull Wololo Londinium Brings RTS Back to the Spotlight

Red Bull Wololo Londinium saw the RTS genre return to esports prominence, drawing over 100k viewers for its Age of Empires II Grand Final.

Royal Ravens Drop Nero After DM Scandal Surfaces Online

Dylan ‘Nero’ Koch has been dropped by the CDL’s Carolina Royal Ravens after a DM controversy emerged on social media.

T1 Turns a Profit for First Time in Organizational History

SK Telecom CS T1 Co., Ltd’s fiscal reports show that T1 has turned a profit for the first time in its organizational history.



https://insider-gaming.com/esports-nations-cup-street-fighter-6/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: IEM Cologne 2026 Major: All Qualified Teams
item.content:encoded:
The invites for the IEM Cologne 2026 Counter-Strike Major are set following the whirlwind conclusion of local LANs as we approached the April 6 cutoff date.

Those local LANs saw heartache and jubilance, as FaZe disastrously missed out on the Major following HLC Pro Belgrade Grand Final defeat to BIG.

On the other hand, the LANs saw a host of new players and new orgs guarantee that they will be immortalized in the highly-prized in-game stickers.

IEM Cologne 2026 Major full invite list:

The IEM Cologne 2026 Major invites* will be applied as follows, from highest to lowest ranking teams across the Europe, Americas, and Asia regions:

Stage 3: 6 EU, 1 Americas, 1 Asia
Stage 2: 5 EU, 3 Americas
Stage 1: 6 EU, 6 Americas, 4 Asia

Stage 3 Invites:

  • Vitality (EU)
  • Natus Vincere (EU)
  • Parivision (EU)
  • Furia (Americas)
  • Aurora (EU)
  • Falcons (EU)
  • MOUZ (EU)
  • The MongolZ (Asia)

Stage 2 Invites:

  • Spirit (EU)
  • Astralis (EU)
  • G2 (EU)
  • FUT (EU)
  • 9z (Americas)
  • paiN (Americas)
  • Monte (EU)
  • Legacy (Americas)

Stage 1 invites:

  • GamerLegion (EU)
  • BIG (EU)
  • BetBoom (EU)
  • B8 (EU)
  • Heroic (EU)
  • Sinners (EU)
  • M80 (Americas)
  • Tyloo (Asia)
  • NRG (Americas)
  • Sharks (Americas)
  • Gaimin Gladiators (Americas)
  • MIBR (Americas)
  • Liquid (Americas)
  • Lynn Vision (Asia)
  • THUNDER dOWNUNDER (Asia)
  • FlyQuest (Asia)

The event consists of three Swiss Stages of 16 teams, starting in Stage 1, followed by eight-team single-elimination playoffs. The event will feature no Best of 1s following ESL’s decision to remove them from the format, with all Swiss matches being Best of 3 contests.

*Note that this list corresponds to the invites as determined by the Valve Regional Standings in the April 6th snapshot of the rankings. The Cologne Major invites must follow this VRS list, but teams will have to confirm acceptance of the invitation and secure the necessary Visas to attend the event.

Visa issues have plagued several tournaments already this year, also affecting last year’s BLAST.tv Austin Major 2025. At Austin, Bestia initially qualified for the event, but were unable to meet the Visa deadline required by BLAST, resulting in Legacy taking their spot at the Major.

If a team is unable to attend, then the next team in that team’s region would be invited. For each region, the next team on the VRS would be: Alliance (EU), BESTIA (Americas), and SemperFi (Asia).

Who do you think will be the surprise package of the IEM Cologne 2026 Major? Let us know on our Discord server!
For more CS news, see how 3DMAX missed out on the Major!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Tuesday, April 7
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Thursday, April 2, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (April 7)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for April 7, 2026.

Across

1 – Informative commercial, for short

4 – Something you trace to draw a Thanksgiving turkey

5 – ___ Johnson, former Prime Minister of the UK

6 – Opposite of include

7 – Crosses (out)

Down

1 – City with the Notre-Dame Cathedral

2 – Bad mood

3 – About eight minutes of the average half-hour sitcom

4 – Remote worker’s office, perhaps

5 – Word that can follow each group of circled letters (and hits at its shape)

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (April 7)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on April 7, 2026.

Across

1 – Public Service Announcement

4 – Home of your fingers

5 – Conservative UK leader during the pandemic

6 – Leave out

7 – Marks the spot

Down

1 – French capital

2 – State of agitation

3 – Products and services shown during an interval on TV

4 – Where the heart is

5 – Amazon packaging

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (April 7)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on April 7, 2026.

Across

1 – PSA

4 – HAND

5 – BORIS

6 – OMIT

7 – XES

Down

1 – PARIS

2 – SNIT

3 – ADS

4 – HOME

5 – BOX

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 7, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 7, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Activity
  • Hint 2: Passion
  • Hint 3: Pastime

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Woodworking
  • Sport
  • Toy
  • Garden
  • Pursuit
  • Fun
  • Collectible
  • Craft
  • Pastime

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 7, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Hobby

A Hobby is an activity that’s pursued for passion, pleasure, or relaxation, as opposed to financial gain or occupation.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on April 8.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • Wordle

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Red Bull Wololo Londinium Brings RTS Back to the Spotlight
item.content:encoded:
Red Bull Wololo Londinium saw the Real Time Strategy (RTS) genre return to esports prominence on Monday, drawing over 100k viewers for its Age of Empires II Grand Final.

The $250,000 event took place at a sold-out Royal Albert Hall, featuring Age of Empires II and Age of Empires IV tournaments as well as a live orchestra providing musical ambience.

The RTS genre was once the genre of esports in the formative years of competitive gaming, with Starcraft and Age of Empires esports tournaments stretching back over 25 years. But the genre’s popularity waned as other titles gained momentum, relegating the RTS genre to a niche interest.

Red Bull Wololo Londinium showed the appeal that remains in the genre as the six-day LAN culminated in its Royal Albert Hall Grand Finals day, with Hamzah ‘Hera’ El-Baher and Alexis ‘MarineLorD’ Eusebi taking home the top prizes after thrilling Grand Finals.

Hera fights back to prove dominance

Red Bull Wololo’s main event saw Hera and Kai ‘Liereyy’ Kallinger duke it out in the Best of 9 Grand Final. The two Team Vitality players renewed their storied rivalry, but it was a rivalry that was recently one-sided in Hera’s favour.

However, the opening games saw Liereyy consistently have the advantages in the early skirmishes, forcing army early and effectively disrupting Hera, crippling his economy.

The challenger was on pace to stun Hera after three games, with victories on the Khitans, Wei, and Cumans. Hera had to find a way to prevent those early rushes from destroying his game.

That was what happened in Game 4 with Hera getting the best of the early game to come out with a substantial villager lead. Liereyy attempted to deal a decisive blow, but couldn’t before Hera could reach the Imperial Age, signalling the end of the contest.

Games 5 and 6 came with increasingly intense brawls, with Hera’s micro acting as the deciding factor on the Mapuche and Byzantines to equalize the series.

After a grindy Game 7, Hera won to score due to the time limit, putting him on the edge of his sixteenth LAN title in a row.

The Canadian once again won the late game in the eighth and final contest, coming back from a 0-3 deficit to once again prove that he is the greatest in the world, claiming $50,000 in the process.

The Grand Final peaked at 109,796 viewers according to viewership aggregator Esports Charts, the second-highest peak for Age of Empires on their records after Red Bull Wololo Legacy 2022, the first Red Bull Wololo event.

MarineLorD wins Age of Empires IV title at Red Bull Wololo

France’s MarineLorD also showed his dominance at Red Bull Wololo Londinium, defeating Daniel ‘Wam01’ Svoboda to claim the $20,000 first prize of the Age of Empires IV tournament.

The Gentle Mates player continues to build his legacy in the title after a 3-2 back-and-forth Grand Final victory.

The Frenchman was declared World champion by his organisation following the victory, which saw him win eight of the last twelve S-Tier events.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
